children.text: identify agency compliance activities to meet the requirements of section 543 of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act () and any other matters relevant to implementing the goals of such Act; and42 U.S.C. 8253
determine if the agency has the internal accounting mechanisms necessary to assess the accuracy and reliability of energy consumption and energy cost figures required under such section.

children.heading: President’s Council on Integrity and Efficiency report to Congress
children.label: b President’s Council on Integrity and Efficiency report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 150 days after , the President’s Council on Integrity and Efficiency shall submit a report to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources and the Committee on Governmental Affairs of the Senate, the Committee on Energy and Commerce, the Committee on Government Operations, and the Committee on Public Works and Transportation of the House of Representatives, on the review conducted by the Inspector General of each agency under this section.October 24, 1992

children.heading: Inspector General review
children.label: c Inspector General review
children.text: Each Inspector General established under is encouraged to conduct periodic reviews of agency compliance with part 3 of title V of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act [ et seq.], the provisions of this subtitle, and other laws relating to energy consumption. Such reviews shall not be inconsistent with the performance of the required duties of the Inspector General’s office.section 402 of title 542 U.S.C. 82511See References in Text note below.1
